---
title: "Tutorials"
description: "Runnable tutorials across the EpiAware packages."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

Every tutorial below is a runnable walkthrough that lives in its package's own documentation, where it is executed and kept in step with the code.
Filter by the [approach](/approaches/index.qmd) it belongs to, or search across all of them.
If you are new to an approach, start with the tutorial marked *start here*.

```{=html}
<div class="filter-table-wrap">
<div class="filter-controls">
  <input type="search" class="filter-search"
         placeholder="Search tutorials" aria-label="Search tutorials">
  <div class="filter-buttons" role="group" aria-label="Filter tutorials by approach">
    <button class="filter-btn is-active" data-category="all">All</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="composed">Composed distributions</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="turing">Composable Turing models</button>
    <button class="filter-btn" data-category="delays">Delays, censoring, modifiers</button>
  </div>
</div>

<table class="data-table">
  <thead>
    <tr><th scope="col">Tutorial</th><th scope="col">What it covers</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr data-category="composed">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/composing-distributions">Composing distributions</a>
        <span class="status-pill status-maturing">start here</span>
      </td>
      <td>The core walkthrough. Build an event tree from named delays, then simulate and fit with the same object. <em>ComposedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="composed">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/linear-chain">Linear chain</a>
      </td>
      <td>The simplest tree, with delays chained in series from onset through to outcome. <em>ComposedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="composed">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/competing-outcomes">Competing outcomes</a>
      </td>
      <td>Where a case's path forks, death or discharge, as a fixed-probability mixture or as racing hazards. <em>ComposedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="composed">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/strata-uncertainty">Strata and uncertainty</a>
      </td>
      <td>Delays that vary across groups, and delays whose parameters are themselves uncertain. <em>ComposedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="composed">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/concepts">Concepts</a>
      </td>
      <td>Maps each modelling idea to the verb that builds it. <em>ComposedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/overview">Overview</a>
        <span class="status-pill status-maturing">start here</span>
      </td>
      <td>How the three roles, prior, infection, and observation, nest through one interface. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/renewal-negbin">Renewal with negative-binomial observations</a>
      </td>
      <td>A renewal infection process fitted to case counts with overdispersed noise. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/realtime-nowcast">Real-time nowcast</a>
      </td>
      <td>Estimating what has happened but not yet been reported, from right-truncated data. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/delays-dayofweek">Delays and day-of-week effects</a>
      </td>
      <td>Layering reporting delays and weekday reporting patterns onto the observation model. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/split-observations">Split observations</a>
      </td>
      <td>Fitting one infection process to more than one observed data stream at once. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/sir-ode">SIR ODE model</a>
      </td>
      <td>Compartmental dynamics as the infection process, written directly. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/case-studies/catalyst-ode">Catalyst ODE model</a>
      </td>
      <td>The same compartmental dynamics, built through Catalyst. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="turing">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/design">Design</a>
      </td>
      <td>The composable design set out in full. <em>ComposableTuringIDModels</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/fitting-with-turing">Fitting with Turing</a>
      </td>
      <td>Estimating a delay distribution from censored, truncated line-list data. <em>CensoredDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/analytical-primarycensored-cdfs">Analytical primary-censored CDFs</a>
      </td>
      <td>Closed-form primary-event censoring, and when it beats numerical integration. <em>CensoredDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/exponentially-tilted-primary-events">Exponentially tilted primary events</a>
      </td>
      <td>Primary events that are not uniform within their window, as in a growing epidemic. <em>CensoredDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/ad-backends">Automatic differentiation backends</a>
      </td>
      <td>Which AD backend to use for censored delay likelihoods, and what it costs. <em>CensoredDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/composed-chains">Composed chains</a>
      </td>
      <td>Modifier leaves used inside a composed event tree, the packages working together. <em>ModifiedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/modifier-pipeline">Modifier pipeline</a>
      </td>
      <td>Rescaling, shifting, and transforming a delay while keeping the distribution interface. <em>ModifiedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/weighted-likelihoods">Weighted likelihoods</a>
      </td>
      <td>Weighting an observation's likelihood, the standard trick for aggregated or count data. <em>ModifiedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr data-category="delays">
      <td class="data-name">
        <a href="https://convolveddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/getting-started/tutorials/visualising-convolutions">Visualising convolutions</a>
      </td>
      <td>Adding delays together, and what the resulting distribution looks like. <em>ConvolvedDistributions</em></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<p class="filter-empty" hidden>No tutorials match that search.</p>
</div>
```

{{< include ../_includes/filter-table.qmd >}}

Have a tutorial you would like to see?
Suggest it on the [forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or see [Contributing](/contributing.qmd).
Analyses built with these tools are in the [Gallery](/gallery.qmd).
